name: Lotka-Volterra with logistic prey carrying-capacity correction
proposal: Extend the lotka-volterra-predator-prey card by replacing the unbounded prey growth term alpha x with a logistic term alpha x (1 - x / K) that saturates at carrying capacity K. The full system becomes dot x = alpha x (1 - x / K) - beta x y; dot y = delta x y - gamma y. In the K -> infinity limit the equation must reduce to the canonical Lotka-Volterra system. The logistic correction destroys the neutral closed-orbit structure and replaces it with a globally attracting equilibrium (at gamma/delta < K) — predator-prey dynamics damp toward steady coexistence rather than persisting as conservative oscillations.
proposedFormulaTeX: \dot{x} = \alpha\, x \left(1 - \frac{x}{K}\right) - \beta\, x\, y, \qquad \dot{y} = \delta\, x\, y - \gamma\, y

dimensional.rhsLabel: alpha x (1 - x/K) - beta x y  [N / T]

limits.name: no_predators
limits.expectedReducesTo: logistic prey growth: dot x = alpha x (1 - x / K) -> stable carrying-capacity equilibrium at x = K

limits.name: coexistence_equilibrium
limits.regime: fixed point with y > 0 and gamma/delta < K
limits.expectedReducesTo: (x*, y*) = (gamma / delta, (alpha / beta) (1 - gamma / (delta K))) — globally asymptotically stable, replacing the neutral centre of the canonical model

conservationLaws.statement: No conserved energy-like quantity. The Volterra invariant V(x, y) = delta x - gamma ln x + beta y - alpha ln y is no longer conserved — d V / dt = -(alpha / K) (x - x*)^2 <= 0, which is precisely a Lyapunov function proving asymptotic stability of the coexistence equilibrium.

references: Verhulst (1838) — Notice sur la loi que la population suit dans son accroissement
Murray — Mathematical Biology I: An Introduction, ch.3 (logistic Lotka-Volterra)
Edelstein-Keshet — Mathematical Models in Biology, ch.6
rationale: The classical Lotka-Volterra closed orbits are structurally unstable — any small biological correction that recognises finite resources destroys them. The logistic prey term is the textbook canonical extension and produces the globally attracting equilibrium that ecologists observe in long-running experiments (Gause, Huffaker). Recording the hypothesis ensures the cross-check engine confirms the K -> infinity limit collapses to the canonical card and that the Lyapunov derivation lines up with the conservation-law claim.
